definition: mai — will not; shall not (archaic/literary)
二度とすまい。 — I will never do it again.
nen — (informal ending particle, often for emphasis or confirmation)
そうだよ、ねん。 — That's right, isn't it?
men — (slang, e.g., 'no way' or suffix for 'guys')
めん、それはないわ。 — No way, that's not happening.
beshi — (archaic auxiliary verb: should, must)
なすべきことはなすべし。 — One must do what must be done.
ito — very; extremely (archaic)
いと美しき花。 — A very beautiful flower.
chinpo — penis (vulgar slang)
この言葉は俗語です。 — This word is slang.
dani — even (archaic/literary)
夢にだに思わなかった。 — I didn't even dream of it.
ide — come on; well then (archaic)
いで、いざ行かん！ — Come on, let's go!
tade — knotweed, smartweed (plant)
蓼食う虫も好き好き。 — There are tastes for everything.
zui — will; intention; as one pleases
彼のずいには従えない。 — I cannot follow his will.
hitsu — chest; box; coffin
古いひつの中から手紙が見つかった。 — A letter was found inside an old chest.
totsu — suddenly; abruptly (often as part of compounds)
彼はとつとして現れた。 — He appeared suddenly.
bekara — should not; must not (archaic/literary auxiliary)
彼は決して嘘をつくべからず。 — He must never lie.
atara — what a pity; alas (archaic/literary)
あたら好機を逸した。 — What a pity to have missed a good opportunity.
nakare — do not; must not (archaic/literary imperative)
嘘をつくなかれ。 — Do not lie.
chigo — infant; child monk (archaic)
昔の絵にはちごが描かれていることが多い。 — Old paintings often depict infants.
game — female duck; (dialectal) turtle
沼にはがめが泳いでいた。 — A female duck was swimming in the swamp.
ikei — strange form;grotesque figure;anomaly
その生物は異形な姿をしていた。 — That creature had a strange form.
gotoshi — like; as if; as though (archaic)
夢のごとし。 — It was like a dream.
geni — truly; indeed (archaic)
げに恐ろしきことかな。 — How truly terrifying it is.
moso — rustlingly;muttering
彼はもそっと何かを言った。 — He muttered something.
tamayura — a moment;a short while
玉響の夢を見た。 — I had a momentary dream.
